additive_batch.reason: the additive/main batch shift explains 0.12-0.42% of program-projection variance, is sign-inconsistent across programs, and cancels in the DiD

confound_rule.statement: a pair is batch_partially_confounded exactly when at least one donor sits in a different replicate at the two endpoints; when every donor keeps its replicate the batch offset attaches to the same donors at both endpoints and cancels in the difference-of-differences

interaction_floor_source.why: the batch-aligned split is the one that carries the batch contrast, so its target x half interaction spread is the floor a confounded temporal call has to clear; the batch-crossed splits carry donor heterogeneity only

not_identifiable.consequence: the batch bias on a Stim48hr endpoint can never be measured directly, only bounded by transfer from Rest/Stim8hr
not_identifiable.reason: batch is perfectly aliased with donor-half in every condition (R1 == {D1,D2}, R2 == {D3,D4} in Rest/Stim8hr) and Stim48hr is all-R2, so no donor is measured in both replicates within a condition; a pure batch effect, and a pure batch x perturbation interaction separated from donor, cannot be isolated in this design - only bounded

sparse_panel_caution_reason: sparse effective panels and near-zero cross-half reproducibility (r ~ 0.0-0.15); th9_like is not stage2-selectable and is listed so it cannot re-enter unflagged
